title: Reading and Use of English - Part 6
description: You are going to read an article from which six sentences have been removed. Choose from the sentences A–G the one which fits each gap (37–42). There is one extra sentence which you do not need to use.

content.title: The City That Never Quiets: Urban Soundscapes and Noise Pollution
content.text: Close your eyes in the centre of any major city and you are immediately immersed in an invisible landscape of sound. Traffic rumbles in every direction, construction machinery hammers in the middle distance, and somewhere overhead an aircraft traces its path across the sky. Most city dwellers have learned to filter this barrage out — but their bodies have not. (37) [37] El ruido urbano afecta a la salud aunque no seamos conscientes de ello. [/37] Research now shows that chronic exposure to urban noise has measurable effects on cardiovascular health, sleep quality and cognitive performance, even when people believe they have adapted to it. || Sound is measured in decibels, and the scale is logarithmic: an increase of ten decibels represents a tenfold increase in intensity. A normal conversation registers around sixty decibels; heavy city traffic typically reaches seventy-five to eighty-five. (38) [38] Los niveles de ruido urbano superan con frecuencia los umbrales seguros. [/38] The World Health Organisation recommends that long-term outdoor noise levels should not exceed fifty-five decibels during the day — a threshold routinely exceeded on most urban streets. || Urban planners and acoustic engineers are developing a range of strategies to address the problem. Traditional noise barriers — the concrete walls beside motorways — are effective but visually unappealing. (39) [39] Las soluciones modernas buscan combinar funcionalidad y estética. [/39] Green infrastructure offers a more attractive alternative: dense hedgerows and tree-lined streets have been shown to reduce noise levels by up to ten decibels while simultaneously improving air quality and biodiversity. || The concept of the 'soundscape' — the total acoustic environment of a place — has shifted how researchers think about noise. Rather than focusing solely on eliminating unwanted sounds, some experts now argue for the active design of positive sounds into urban environments. (40) [40] Añadir sonidos positivos puede mejorar la percepción del entorno urbano. [/40] The sound of moving water has proved particularly effective: fountains and small urban streams can mask traffic noise and significantly improve the reported wellbeing of people nearby. || Quieter streets also have economic implications. Studies have found that properties on particularly noisy roads sell for measurably less than equivalent homes on quieter streets. (41) [41] El ruido afecta negativamente al valor de los inmuebles. [/41] Businesses in noisy areas report lower footfall, and outdoor dining — an increasingly important revenue stream for urban restaurants — is difficult where traffic noise dominates. || Ultimately, reducing urban noise requires both technical solutions and changes in behaviour. (42) [42] La solución requiere esfuerzos colectivos e individuales. [/42] Cities that have introduced lower speed limits, extended pedestrian zones and restricted the noisiest vehicles from residential areas have seen measurable improvements — proof that quieter cities are achievable, if we choose to pursue them.

paragraphs.A: Newer approaches attempt to integrate noise reduction into designs that are also visually pleasant and ecologically valuable.
paragraphs.B: Residents who suffer from excessive noise are significantly more likely to experience anxiety and reduced concentration at work.
paragraphs.C: Planning authorities have begun requiring acoustic impact assessments before approving major developments near residential areas.
paragraphs.D: This disparity creates a clear financial incentive for local authorities to invest in noise reduction programmes.
paragraphs.E: Birdsong, rustling leaves and the gentle sound of water all contribute to what researchers call a restorative acoustic environment.
paragraphs.F: No single authority or technology can solve it alone; collaboration between governments, designers and citizens is essential.
paragraphs.G: Busy arterial roads in London, Paris and New York routinely record levels twenty or more decibels above this safe limit.

questions.explanation: B introduce los efectos físicos y cognitivos del ruido, que el artículo desarrolla como investigación en la siguiente oración.
G proporciona datos concretos de ciudades reales que superan el límite de la OMS, reforzando la afirmación anterior sobre umbrales.
A resume las soluciones modernas que combinan reducción acústica con valor estético, antes de describir la infraestructura verde.
E da ejemplos de sonidos positivos (canto de pájaros, agua) que completan la idea de diseñar entornos acústicos restauradores.
D explica la implicación económica que crea incentivos para que las autoridades inviertan en reducir el ruido.
F introduce la necesidad de colaboración colectiva antes de los ejemplos de políticas municipales exitosas.
